_meta.name: TikTok For Business — Design Tokens
_meta.source: ../../TikTok_guidelines.pdf — TikTok For Business Brand Guidelines (70pp PDF)
_meta.method: pdftotext (exact written values) + pdftoppm PNG render read with vision; extended palette hexes sampled from a 300dpi render of page 35 (image-only swatches, no printed values).
_meta.notes: The brand book states 'Do not create new colors.' The 4 PRIMARY brand colors below are the canonical, written-in-doc values (with Pantone/RGB/CMYK), and the doc ALSO sanctions an extended/illustrative palette (p.35) for secondary/illustration use only — so the rule is '4 PRIMARY brand colors + a sanctioned extended/illustrative palette,' not 'only 4 colors.' The 'derived' neutrals/state tints are NOT new brand colors — they are functional UI surfaces (tints/shades of the doc's own Black/White) needed to build accessible web pages, kept deliberately neutral so brand colors stay the only chromatic accents per the doc's 'one brand color per composition' + 'create focus with black or white' rules.

extendedPalette.flag: SECONDARY / ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY — NOT for primary composition surfaces. Per the brand book, primary color usage is limited to Razzmatazz, Splash, Black, and White; the extended palette is reserved for illustration/illustrative use (p.35, referenced again on the Illustration page).
extendedPalette.note: Image-only swatches on page 35 (no printed hex/Pantone/CMYK values). Hex values below were SAMPLED from a 300dpi render of page 35 and are APPROXIMATE. The palette is presented as 3 tonal rows (light tint / mid-tone / dark shade) of 11 hue columns spanning the spectrum (~22 primary swatches plus the lighter tint row). The mid-tone row includes the canonical brand hues themselves (Splash #25F4EE and a Razzmatazz-equivalent), which confirms sampling accuracy.

mid.name: Mid — Splash
mid.note: Sampled exactly equal to the canonical Splash hex.

usageBySurface.source: Doc p.35: 'Use RGB color values for all digital applications. Use Pantone color values when printing stationery or logo to ensure correct branding color. Use CMYK color values for all other print applications.'

usage.razzmatazz: Primary brand color. Full-bleed section backgrounds, primary CTA buttons, and to highlight ONE keyword/phrase in a headline (the only color allowed to emphasize text on black OR white).
usage.splash: Secondary/accent. Full-bleed section backgrounds, borders/strokes, image-frame borders, decorative shapes. MUST NOT highlight text (fails accessibility). On Splash, foreground text/icons are Black.
usage.black: Default page background and default text on white. Pairs with one brand color to create focus.
usage.white: Default text on dark, card/surface fill on dark layouts, and the light-mode page background.
usage.neutrals: Greys are for UI structure only (borders, muted captions, light panels). Per the doc, never use grey/neutral as a primary composition background — reserve full-bleed for Black, White, Razzmatazz, or Splash.
usage.extended: The extended palette (p.35) is for SECONDARY/ILLUSTRATIVE use only (illustration, charts/diagrams), never for primary composition surfaces. Primary composition still limited to Razzmatazz, Splash, Black, White.
usage.rule: Limit to one brand color per composition; pair it with black or white. Two colors max in a single text treatment (e.g. Black + Razzmatazz or White + Razzmatazz).

families.heading: 'Sofia Pro', Spartan, 'League Spartan', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
families.emphasis: 'Sofia Pro Soft', 'Baloo 2', 'Sofia Pro', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

adLockup.family: DIN Next LT Pro Bold Condensed
adLockup.use: The 'Don't Make Ads.' campaign lockup typeface (Advertising / Graphics rules, p.58). A real SECOND brand typeface used only for the 'Don't Make Ads.' lockup — distinct from Sofia Pro / Sofia Pro Soft.
adLockup.rules: 'Don't Make Ads.' is always set in DIN Next LT Pro Bold Condensed on a black background with white type; animates/appears word-by-word for emphasis; left-aligned only (never right or center aligned).
adLockup.source: Doc p.58 (Graphics): "'Don't Make Ads.' should always be DIN Next LT Pro - Bold Condensed."

brandFamilies.note: Sofia Pro = headlines/body primary; Sofia Pro Soft = emphasis only, in combination with Sofia Pro; DIN Next LT Pro Bold Condensed = 'Don't Make Ads.' ad lockup only. Current CSS uses League Spartan only for headline/display fallback and Roboto for body fallback to avoid an all-display-page feel. Doc-approved free web substitutes: Spartan & Roboto (Google), Helvetica/Arial (system), SF Pro (Apple). Sofia Pro Soft substitutes: Houschka Round, SF Pro Rounded, Arial Rounded — closest free Google option is Baloo 2. DIN Next LT Pro Bold Condensed free web subs: Oswald / Archivo Narrow.

tracking.headline: -0.02em
tracking.caption: 0.01em
tracking.note: Doc specifies -10pt tracking for headline/subhead/body and +10pt for caption.

rules.do: Emphasize keywords with Razzmatazz + Sofia Pro Soft. Left or center align. Emphasize the trailing punctuation along WITH the highlighted keyword/phrase — the punctuation that follows a highlighted keyword should also be highlighted (p.31).
rules.dont: No all-caps headlines, no all-lowercase headlines, no gradients/shadows on type, no stacked type, no distortion, never use Bold weight for body copy, keep sizes clearly distinct. No images or patterns inside type (don't put pictures or patterns in type). Tracking is exactly -10pt (not a range) — don't track too tightly OR too loosely. Don't over-lead OR under-lead — always refer to the type system (Headline/Subhead/Body 120%, Body/Caption per scale).
rules.emphasizeTrailingPunctuation: When highlighting a keyword/phrase, also highlight the punctuation that immediately follows it (p.31).
rules.trackingExact: Tracking is exactly -10pt, not a range — applies to headline, subhead, and body (caption is +10pt) (p.30, p.33).
rules.leadingRule: Do not lead too much and do not lead too little — refer to the type system (headline/subhead 120%, body 140% / per scale) (p.33).
rules.noImagesOrPatternsInType: Do not put pictures or patterns inside type (p.33).

localization.note: In regions with accented languages, set leading (line height) to at least 120% of the font size so accents do not collide (e.g. 36pt font → 43.2pt leading) (p.32).

spaceNote: 8px rhythm (the design tool kit's 'Min Circle Diameter' steps of 20/30/50px and the 4pt type minimums imply an 8px-based grid). Section vertical padding uses the larger steps (96/128px).

radius.source: Doc: rounded-rectangle roundness limited to 25%, 50%, 100%. 100% roundness reserved for CTA / word-bubble stadium shapes. Image masks use 25% or 50% roundness.

rules.mixedCornersAllowed: Up to two DIFFERENT curve proportions may be used within the same element (e.g. a rounded-rect mixing 25% and 50% corners is allowed) (p.41).
rules.offBookFlag: Only 25%, 50%, and 100% roundness are sanctioned by the brand book. The px radii here (--radius-sm 8px, md 16px, lg 24px) and ANY non-25/50/100% radius are DESIGN-SYSTEM EXTRAPOLATIONS (off-book) for web UI chrome, not brand-book values. Use the % roundness values for brand-faithful shapes; reserve the px radii for utilitarian UI only.

shadow.name: none
shadow.note: Brand book: 'Do not apply drop shadows or other effects.' Depth is created with flat color blocks + layered shapes, NOT shadows.

shapes.note: Tool kit shapes (bubbles, rounded rectangles, patterns, emphasis marks) derive from the two circles in the TikTok logo. 100% stadium bubbles are for CTAs / word-holding bubbles, not generic labels. Images may rotate in 10deg increments.

bubble.description: Word-holding stadium pill — the two logo circles extended into a rounded-rectangle (stadium) shape in order to hold words. 100% roundness (fully rounded ends).
bubble.primaryUse: call to action (CTA) across digital assets; may also add emphasis on print assets
bubble.source: Doc p.40 (Bubbles): derived from the two logo circles, extended into a rounded rectangle to hold words; fill & stroke variations in Splash, Razzmatazz, Black; digital min 30px / print min .2in; primarily used as CTA.

roundedRectangles.primaryUse: image masks with colored borders; may stretch to accommodate image height/width
roundedRectangles.graphicUse: layer different-color rounded rectangles to create focus; do not stack rounded rectangles of the same color; do not use 100% roundness alone as a graphic element.
roundedRectangles.source: Doc p.41-42 (Rounded Rectangles): fill/stroke in Splash, Razzmatazz, Black, White; roundness limited to 25%, 50%, 100%; up to two curve proportions in one element; reserve 100% for digital CTAs or layered graphic use.

patterns.use: backgrounds/dividers — bring visual interest as graphic elements, divide and organize content, used full-bleed or masked within
patterns.implementation: CSS patterns must be flat mask/SVG dot tiles, not CSS gradient functions, so the implementation matches the no-gradient brand rule.
patterns.contrast: only on high-contrast (black or white) or non-matching-color fields; may also go on Splash/Razzmatazz backgrounds ONLY if the pattern does not itself include those colors. Never place text on top of patterns; never place patterns on images, competing/clashing colors, or similar/same colors.
do: Use patterns to divide content and create flow.
dont: Do not place patterns on top of images.
Do not place patterns on competing/clashing colors.
Do not place patterns on similar/same colors (the shapes get lost).
Do not place text on top of patterns.
patterns.source: Doc p.43-44 (Patterns / Pattern Application): min circle diameter 20px (50px for the larger pattern scale); derived from the logo circles.

emphasisMarks.libraryNote: Brand book labels this the 5-variant Emphasis Marks library: Cross, Chevron, Breadcrumbs, Bracket (Round & Square), Mixed Cross — bracket counts as round + square, giving the six tokens listed above.

rules.onEveryH1: Emphasis Marks should be added to ALL Level 1 (H1) headlines using the Emphasis Mark library.
rules.twoMarksBracketingHeadline: Two marks are preferred — one at the beginning and one at the end of the headline — and the beginning and end marks should be DIFFERENT colors (e.g. Splash, Razzmatazz, Black, or White).
rules.squareOnlyAtSentenceEnd: Use only SQUARE marks at the end of a sentence (to avoid confusion with punctuation); do NOT use curved/round Emphasis Marks at the end of a sentence.
rules.sizedToHeadlineXHeight: The x-height of the headline font is the unit of measurement for the alignment and size of the Emphasis Marks — never scale a mark larger or smaller than the headline font x-height, and do not ignore alignment guidelines.

emphasisMarks.source: Doc p.45-46 (Emphasis Marks + Things to Avoid).

video.note: Videos should always be shot in a vertical 9:16 format to stay true to the platform; never horizontal or square (p.52).

adFormats.note: Advertising format sizes: social stills at 4:5 and 1:1, static banner 350x200, YouTube pre-roll 16:9, social stories video 9:16 (p.56-57).
adFormats.source: Doc p.56-57 (Advertising Guidelines — Video / Still / Social Rules).

logo.noTypedLogo: Do not recreate the logo by typing it with a font. Use an official logo asset only; if none is supplied, reference TikTok For Business in ordinary copy, not as a logo/wordmark.
logo.minSizeNote: Horizontal smallest size 5px tall (.07in / .2mm); vertical smallest size 14px tall (.2in / 5mm) (p.16).
logo.clearspaceNote: Clearspace around the logo equals the cap height of the 'T' for both horizontal and vertical lockups (p.17).
logo.colorVariationsNote: Four color variations. Full-color logo only on black or white backgrounds (white logo on dark, black logo on light). Monochrome for simpler needs (p.15).

rotation.note: Logo may be rotated for playfulness in 10-degree increments and must never end up upside down; when rotated vertically (90 deg), place 'TikTok' at the bottom to ground the logo (p.21).

onColor.note: Black and white logos can be used on a Razzmatazz background; on a Splash background use the BLACK logo only — do not use the white logo on Splash (p.15).

logo.iconRule: two-circle icon only when full name also present
logo.iconRuleNote: The two circles can be used as an icon only if 'TikTok For Business' also appears on the page (e.g. a social icon next to the profile handle) (p.14).

placement.note: Approved logo placements; when they cannot be met, follow clearspace and minimum scale guidelines for maximum accessibility (p.20).

animation.note: Follow color, scale, clearspace, placement, and rotation guidelines when animating. Movement should feel efficient, smooth, fun; use motion blur, rotation, and scale. Do NOT add drop shadows or gradients; do NOT distort/manipulate the logo on end states (glitch / squash-and-stretch acceptable only during transitions) (p.22-23).

dont: Don't re-create the logo by typing it with a font.
Don't stretch, distort, or manipulate the logo.
Don't add effects like drop shadows or gradients.
Don't place the logo on a background that reduces its legibility.
Don't outline or create a keyline around the logo.
logo.source: Doc p.13-25 (Logo: Lockups, Social Lockups, Color Variations, Scale, Clearspace, Placement, Rotation, Animation, Things to Avoid).
